# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on self-hosted runners in our datacenter. The workflow executes custom build scripts and deployment commands provided by developers. This gives our team flexibility to run any build process they need on our infrastructure.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD Workflow for Custom Builds & Deployments 
on: [push, pull_request] # Specify what event will trigger the workflow (e.g., push to main branch or changes in a PR)  
jobs:
  build-and-deploy: 
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest     # Run on latest version of Ubuntu if you have one available, otherwise use self hosted runner depending upon your configuration and requirements         
                                # Specify the commands to run for each job step (e.g., installing dependencies or executing script)  
    steps:                      
      - name: Checkout code 
        uses: actions/checkout@v2     # Use action 'actions' with version v2 which is recommended because it automatically sets up node-versions, caching and fetching your repository at versions (uses the latest as default) of npm packages. It also allows you to use pull requests in branch restrictions
        
      - name: Run build script  # Specify steps here for building code or whatever process needs done before deployment using provided command by developers  
        run : '<command_to-be-provided>'   
                                      
                                   
      
                                    
                            
     - name: Deploy to production (this will vary based on the type of deployments)  # Specify steps here for performing a set operation or any other tasks after deployment using provided command by developers  
        run : '<command_to-be-provided>'     
                                      
                                           .env.production is your example environment file and you need to replace the <command_to-be-filled>  with actual commands used for deploying changes into production server
